titleStmt.title: A Transcription of the text of John from Canon Tables in Poitiers BM 17
VL39
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project in 2008 as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers extracts of ff. 2r-15r. Only the text of John in the canon tables has been transcribed. However, because of the repetition of verses, the page breaks in this transcription relate to the chapter breaks of John rather than the pagination of the manuscript. The transcription includes the text of John from the canon tables for collation purposes. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

body.note: Letter of Eusebius to Carpianus (Ammonius quidam) and the titles of the canons.
Continuation of the Letter of Eusebius and title of Canon I.

ab.note: This text probably derives from Matt. 3:3, not John.
This text probably derives from Matt. 3:11, not John.
This text probably derives from Matt. 3:16, not John.
This text may derive from Luke 23:23, not John.
Canon II (Mt Mc Lc) covers folio 4v to 6r.
This text also has the reference Matt. 3:7, but John offers the closer match.
This text also has the reference Matt. 11:27, but John offers the closer match.
The canon number has been swapped with that of the next line: it should read xxx.
The canon number has been swapped with that of the previous line: it should read cxiiii.
This text also has the reference Matt. 11:27, but John offers the closer match.
This text also has the reference Matt. 12:9, but John offers the closer match.
This text also has the reference Matt. 20:28, but John offers the closer match.
This text also has the reference Matt. 21:22, but John offers the closer match.
This line was originally omitted and squeezed in between the lines, apparently by the first hand.
Canon V (Matt. Luc.) covers folio 7v to 8v.
Canon VI (Matt. Marc.) covers folio 9r and most of 9v.
Canon VIII (Luc. Marc.) covers the first half of folio 10r.
This text also has the reference Luke 22:32, but John offers the closer match.
This text also has the reference Luke 23:4, but John offers a close match.
This text also has the reference Luke 24:36, but John offers the closer match.
This text also has the reference Luke 24:41, but John offers the closer match.
Canon X Matt. covers folio 10v to 11v.
Canon X Marc. and the beginning of Canon X Luc. are found on folio 11v.
The first half of the page is the conclusion of Canon X Lucas.
If the reading were simply 'manens', it is hard to see why it should be abbreviated.
Finiunt canon(es) euangelior(um) iuxta eusebium ep(iscopu)m. (This is followed by the incipit of Jerome's Epistula ad Damasum, which begins on folio 15v).
